dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3225 IS: Fishing Industry Safety, Health, and Wellness Improvement Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Markey (for himself, Mr. Sullivan, Ms. Collins, Mr. King, Mr. Merkley, Ms. Murkowski, Mrs. Shaheen, and Mr. Wicker) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation

form.official-title: To amend the Fishing Safety Training Grants and Fishing Safety Research Grant programs.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Fishing Industry Safety, Health, and Wellness Improvement Act of 2025.

section.header: Fishing safety training and research grants programs
section.text: Section 4502 of title 46, United States Code, is amended—

clause.text: in subparagraph (A)(ii), by striking and after the semicolon;
by redesignating subparagraph (B) as subparagraph (C); and
by inserting after subparagraph (A) the following:

subparagraph.text: to conduct safety and prevention training that addresses behavioral and physical health risks, that include substance use disorder and worker fatigue, facing fishing vessel operators and crewmembers; and

clause.text: by striking , in consultation with and based on criteria established by the Commandant of the Coast Guard; and
by striking subsection on a competitive basis. and inserting “subsection—

subparagraph.text: based on criteria developed in consultation with the Commandant of the Coast Guard.
in paragraph (4), by striking $3,000,000 for fiscal year 2023 and inserting to the Secretary of Health and Human Services $6,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2025 through 2029; and

in paragraph (1), by inserting , and on understanding and mitigating behavioral and physical health risks, that include substance use disorder and worker fatigue, facing members of the commercial fishing industry after weather detection;

clause.text: by striking , in consultation with and based on criteria established by the Commandant of the Coast Guard,; and
by striking subsection on a competitive basis. and inserting the following: “subsection—

subparagraph.text: based on criteria developed in consultation with the Commandant of the Coast Guard.
in paragraph (4), by striking $3,000,000 for fiscal year 2023 and inserting to the Secretary of Health and Human Services $6,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2025 through 2029.
